meaning: adj.荳榊庄蜴溯ｰʒ噪,荳榊庄螳ｽ諱慕噪
examples: I hope not so. Imprudence or thoughtlessness in money matters would be unpardonable in me.
Euthanasia is a special kind of unpardonable crime in the name of charity, guilty up to the hilt!
Perhaps the most unpardonable sin: 50% of smartphone users admit to checking Facebook while at a movie.
In his third season, he went 19-16 and missed the NCAA tournament again, an unpardonable offense at Carolina.
